item.title: Savannah Guthrie and the Hard Truth About True Crime
item.description: Everyone wants a happy outcome. But sometimes, the greatest relief is any ending at all.
item.dc:creator: Megan Garber

item.title: Venezuela Seems to Be Going … Well?
item.description: The administration’s plan is working, but democracy remains elusive.
item.dc:creator: Missy Ryan

item.title: Wrestling’s Newest Star Is Massive, Bearded, and Ready to Piledrive ICE
item.description: The debate over immigration enforcement has crept into a brash and crass entertainment, which is less immune to reality than you’d think.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Gordon

item.title: What Happened After a Teacher Ditched Screens
item.description: Why one early adopter of computers in classrooms has decided to toss them
item.dc:creator: Jenny Anderson

item.title: The MAGA Intellectual Who Prophesied a Queen Melania
item.description: An ally of J. D. Vance who has dabbled in monarchism is now working for Viktor Orbán.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker

item.title: She Testified About Being Raped. Then ICE Showed Up.
item.description: Immigration officers are making arrests in sensitive locations, including family court.
item.dc:creator: Marie-Rose Sheinerman

item.title: Trump Is Putting America’s Weaknesses on Display
item.description: What China can learn about the limits of American military capacity
item.dc:creator: Phillips Payson O’Brien

item.title: A Social Experiment Within an Airbnb
item.description: Saturday Night Livecaptured the nightmare of misunderstanding personal boundaries.
item.dc:creator: Paula Mejía

item.title: Dreams in April
item.dc:creator: Traci Brimhall

item.title: The Man Holding MAHA Together
item.description: Can RFK Jr.’s book publisher keep the movement alive?
item.dc:creator: Tom Bartlett

item.title: The Real Intelligence Failure in Iran
item.description: A costly quagmire was predictable. Trump went to war anyway.
item.dc:creator: Shane Harris

item.title: Why Trump Thinks He Can Walk Away From the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. is feeling much less pain than its allies are.
item.dc:creator: Idrees Kahloon

item.title: I Took to the Road and Found Hope for America
item.description: At sites devoted to preserving the nation’s complicated past, people were constructing better futures.
item.dc:creator: Beverly Gage

item.title: What Trump’s Address to the Nation Revealed
item.description: Panelists onWashington Week With The Atlanticjoined to discuss the president’s first national address since the war with Iran began.
item.dc:creator: The Editors

item.title: How Some People Became So Averse to Hype
item.description: The more everyone loves something, the less you might want to join in.
item.dc:creator: Rafaela Jinich

item.title: The Black Women Changing the DAR
item.description: More and more women of color are joining the 136-year-old organization.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Lawler

item.title: The Endless Goodbye
item.description: More than a decade before my dad died, I lost him to dementia.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Parker

item.title: What an Ivy League Education Really Gets You
item.description: Economists have a new theory of why graduates of top colleges have so much career success.
item.dc:creator: Rose Horowitch

item.title: The Iranian Opposition’s Urgent Task
item.description: A fractious movement is coming to recognize the need for common ground.
item.dc:creator: Arash Azizi

item.title: What Trump Wants From Cuba
item.description: A conversation with Vivian Salama about who stands to gain if America overhauls the island’s leadership
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen
